title: Hymenocallis patens
taxonomy: Hymenocallis patens is classified in the family Amaryllidaceae, which includes various flowering plants commonly known as the lily family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its showy flowers and underground storage organs. The genus Hymenocallis is further divided into several species, including H. patens, which is native to the southeastern United States. Hymenocallis patens is a perennial plant with strap-like leaves and white, funnel-shaped flowers that bloom in late summer. Its classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other members of the Amaryllidaceae family.

intro: Hymenocallis patens, also known as the spider lily, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Amaryllidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of H. patens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with H. patens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other upper respiratory infections.
